feed.title: SAP Community - SAP ERP
feed.subtitle: SAP ERP Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Easy Expert functionality does not contain specific characteristic of a Value Assignment
entry.content:
We are looking to add GB Validity Area to existing instances based on certain Value Assignments as instructed by our Product Safety and Regulatory Affairs dept. I plan to utilize Easy Expert functionality to do this.

However on checking the functionality, I've encountered a problem to one specific Value Assignment: Hazard Ingredients.

I accessed Easy Expert function via MORE > UTILITIES > SETS OF RULES > NEW while in t/code CG02 when in a spec.

In the Value Assignment section, I selected Change Existing Value Assignment and enter into the Multiple Selection button to specify my requirements.

Specifically Hazard Ingredients, the only field listed is REMARKS on the Easy Expert Characteristic Changes box.

Is there a way to include other Characteristics to a specific Value Assignment currently not present within the Easy Expert function, please?

I plan to include an additional GB Validity entry to existing instances but only based on specific Hazard Comp rule entry that appears in SOURCE NOTE characteristic.

(attachments included)

We need to have this option otherwise it will have a negative impact on our data load plans on our live system.

entry.title: Year-end closing for non-leading ledger in New GL
entry.content:
Hi, 

Can someone please explain the process of carry forward customer/vendor (F.07) for non-leading ledger only in ECC6.0

The Fiscal year for non-leading ledger is from Jan to Dec and FY for leading ledger is April to March. Understand that FAGLGVTR should be executed to perform the balance carryforward of G/L Balance, but my question is how to perform the balance carry forward process for vendor/customer (F.07) for non-leading ledger in Dec to get the report correctly for Local reporting since this process is not ledger specific. 

Once the next fiscal year (say 2025) is opened for non-leading ledger, will the values of leading ledger automatically reflect in non-leading or how...?

Best regards,
Balam

entry.title: Does SAP Need Root Access and sudo
entry.content:
Hi,

 

   We are currently reviewing our security posture and would like to get clarification with SAP on the following:

  1. Does SAP need root access for SAP installation (SUM , SWPM )and OS patching for linux in Hyperscaler ? 
  2. What is the recommended best practice from SAP with regards to root linux user access ? Is there documentation to a root alternative like ccloud user or equivalent in hyperscaler ?
  3. What is SAP recommended best practice for sudo bash control and user case? Is it PERSON_NAME to use sudo for SUM, SWPM and OS Patching for SAP system on Linu

 

Thanks


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Hotkey Macro for SAP Applications
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m looking for software recommendations to help automate some repetitive tasks in my workflow. Specifically, I need a tool that can:

  • Control programs through automatic text inputs or mouse clicks.
  • Book predefined materials to a project.
  • Book work activities (A, B, or C) of an employee (variable employee X, Y, or Z) to projects.

Since the materials, employees, and activities are always the same, I would like to store these in a table as variables and retrieve them with a hotkey.

In Past we used AutoHotkey for this Szenario. But due to some policy issues we are looking for an alternative Software. Is there an SAP product or external?

Any suggestions for software that can handle these requirements would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Error HUGENERAL066 in LI20 During Inventory Write-Off
entry.content:
Hello All,

We are encountering an issue during the Physical Inventory (PI) process in transaction LI20 when attempting to write off inventory. The system generates error HUGENERAL066:

"Quantity 0 (not packed) is less than the requested quantity 4."

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Go into transaction LI20.
  2. Enter the inventory record and Warehouse, Variance in %: 10
  3. Click on “Write Off”.
  4. The following error message appears:
    • Quantity 0 (not packed) is less than the requested quantity 4.
    • Message No.: HUGENERAL066

Do you have any suggestions on how to resolve this error? Is there a configuration, process step, or master data issue that could be causing the discrepancy?

Thank you in advance for your support and insights.

entry.title: SAP_IDOC_STATUSES
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

In SAP ,How an Idoc moves from one status code to another?

What is the  sequence of idoc status codes?

I am not talking about manually changing Idoc status by RC1_IDOC_SET_STATUS.

entry.title: SAP_IDOC_PROCESSING_PROGRAMS
entry.content:
SAP_IDOC_PROCESSING_PROGRAMS

What is the Functionality of the below Reports in SAP?
RBDMANI2
RBDAGAI2
RBDSYNEI
RBDAPP01
RBDAGAIE
RBDAGAIN
RBDSYNEO
RSEOUT00

entry.title: How to find seriel number wise stock and seriel number wise status?
How to schedule SAP certification exam without Learning hub subscription.
entry.content:
Hi,

Recently I tried to schedule exam for SAP Certification C_TS452_2410 from learning.sap.com, I found that to schedule exam you need to subscribe SAP learning hub which cost around USD1392.

Is there any way to schedule exam without learning hub subscription ?

Please help me out !

Thank you.

Sujit

entry.title: BDLS - ORA-00001
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

We need to run BDLS in our ERP testsystem. At the end of the conversion, the job terminates with the following errors:

1. ORA-00001: unique constraint (UNKNOWN.obj#=2798522) violated
    Segment type ORA-00001: unique constraint (UNKNOWN.obj#=2798522 not defined for message type      ) violated

2.   Table    Field         Number of records

      EDPP1 PARNUM 1

How can we solve this issue? Please assist.

Thank you!!!

Best Regards

Jürgen

entry.title: Fixed assets
entry.content:
Looking for SAP Assets accounting experts  Insights: AS91 Upload asset balance on current fiscal year .

Hi Network,

Currently facing an issue while uploading asset balances through AS91 in SAP. When I click on "Transfer Values," the system automatically picks the capitalization date as transfer date as the 

Assuming that going-live date is 01.06.2025 , and need to upload balances as of 01.06.2025.

however the following parameter is changed as the following :-

Transfer Date/Last Closed Fiscal Year: 30-05-2024
Last Period Posted in Previous System (Transfer During FY): 05-2024

* in case of keeping the start depreciation date as capitalization date it calculates the first 5 month of the current fiscal and added to the depreciation of the June depreciation.

Has anyone encountered this issue before or know how to resolve it? Any insights, solutions, or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: OBXL setting- payment small differences are posted with Tax line/tax code
entry.content:
Dear team, we are facing a challenge in posting the small customer payment differences to specified GL account maintained in tcode OBXL. The issue is when we post customer payment(F-28) the small difference which is under tolerance getting posted by tax code hence posting is generating separate tax line for small difference. 

The same testing I have performed in client. But in this client the customer payment posting is not calculating tax and hence not generating separate tax line.

The requirement is that we do not want to calculate tax on small differences amount and if possible, want to use default tax code for small differences.

I have also checked the GL setting under control data (FS00) for both clients. The setting is same, and the used tax code is tax applicable

entry.title: Information regarding the ECC6.0 EHP 0
entry.content:
I need the information regarding the SAP ECC 6.0 EHP 0
which type of SLES VERSION AND SPS VERSION is supported for SAP ECC 6.0 EHP 0

entry.title: VL10C Save 1 by 1
entry.content:
Hello

We are searching for a solution for our VL10C creation job. We are running ECC on S4H

Current scenario is Vl10C determines all relevant orders and try to create Outbound delivery and Commit the save all at once.

On the "old " ecc Without S4H  the Vl10c job created the deliveries 1 by 1 and we could see the amount of created deliveries increasing.

At this moment we have For example 3500 salesorders in vl10c relevant for Outbound delivery creation, but the background job is running for several hours but no outbound delivery is created.

We are looking for a setting which we can use , That the deliveries will be created 1 by 1 

we have a  background job running with program RVV50R10C

entry.title: Why are the "Screen" fields grayed out in Screen Structure for extended view menu?
entry.content:
I have attempted to follow the instructions in this blog post to add a custom field to quality notifications: User-Fields in PM/CS/QM Notifications : Screen-Exi... - SAP Community

I have deviated slightly from the instructions, as an enhancement project using enhancement QQMA0001 had already been created in the system, and some custom tabs/fields already added. Selecting an existing custom tab, I tried to add another subscreen, but the "Screen" fields are all greyed out. Why can't I use the "screen" fields below the first one?

entry.title: Session of forex revaluation and translation in SM35 unable to process automatically in SAP ECC
entry.content:
Hi,

Our BU team has request to automate Forex revaluation and translation monthly.

And currently we have tried to set up the schedule job via SM37 in the testing environment. However, after the job successfully executed in SM37, the session in SM35 does not process automatically. The status was New instead of In Process

Kindly advise if any step that we have missed out. Appreciate if someone can assist to advise it.

 

Thank you,

KC

entry.title: Differences in SAP configuration steps required for Build-to-Order (BTO) compared to MTO or ATO?
entry.content:
I have implemented MTO (Make-to-Order) and ATO (Assemble-to-Order). I used planning strategy 20 for MTO and 81/82 for ATO.
I am unsure how BTO (Build-to-Order) differs from ATO or MTO, or if it's the same thing. The key differences I've identified are:
  • MTO is the broadest term, encompassing all production triggered by a customer order.
     
  • BTO emphasizes customization and configuration during the production process.
     
  • ATO involves a combination of pre-production and final assembly based on customer orders.
What different SAP configuration steps are required for BTO? And how do these steps differentiate it from MTO or ATO?

entry.title: Add item only for costing in production BOM
entry.content:
Hi 

We have a process order BOM where we add a tiny amount of liquid created in house by another department that does not use SAP. So we don't want the liquid in the BOM to create a purchase requisition or production order. We just need the value of the liquid to be added to the process order's cost. 

How is this possible? I have tried creating the material as a type NLAG, added it to BOM as type N - non-stock item. But then the process order just seems to ignore it. I cant see it in the costing analysis.

entry.title: PY-US-NT-GR- auto-assigned incorrectly when creating IT0194
entry.content:
Hello, We've identified an issue within the SAP garnishment module when creating IT0194, where the internal number isn't being auto-assigned correctly. For instance, when creating a new garnishment record for employee 20112865, the system is auto-populating an existing internal number (0002), which is already active and has deductions. This leads to the existing record being overwritten when the expectation was for a new number (0006) to be assigned.

Additionally, we've discovered that the garnishment processor can input a subtype from the PA30 Maintain HR Master Data screen in SAP, which is causing complications with garnishment record setups. This unexpected capability needs to be addressed, and we plan to raise a note with SAP to investigate why this is allowed and how it can be corrected.

Using the same employee example, the issue arose when a specialist entered "1" in the subtype field before accessing the garnishment module. This action prevents the record number from being auto-assigned, leading to cascading effects on subsequent garnishment setups, as seen with the initial example.

We attempted to correct this by updating the record from 1 to 0001 in SAP"s backend. However, creating a new record still resulted in the original issue, where an active internal record number was mistakenly overwritten, causing incorrect delimiting of the existing record.

If you would like to look at our employee in our test system (S03), please let me know and I get our security team to open up the system to you but before I do that wanted to get your input first, just incase you already know about this issue we are having.

Sincerely,

Bonnie Bazargan 

 

This situation presents a compliance risk and needs urgent resolution.

entry.title: CM25 - Do not allow orders to start so close to a non-working day that they cant finish
entry.content:
Hi 

When I look in CM25, a lot of non-dispatched orders look longer than they really are, because they suggest to start them at a time where they can't be finished before a non-working day. Is there a setting somewhere to stop the system from doing this? I bet there is somewhere maybe in the strategy but I cant find it.
